patient-resources.title: Patient Resources
patient-resources.intro: Helpful guides, forms, and clinical documents to support your care journey at Middletown Medical. All resources are published by our clinical staff and reflect current evidence-based standards of care.

cards.title: Colorectal Cancer Screening
cards.description: Evidence-based guidelines for colorectal cancer (CRC) screening and surveillance intervals for patients at average risk, starting at age 45. Includes the recommended Fecal FIT screening protocol, colonoscopy surveillance schedules, and criteria for identifying high-risk patients who require earlier or more frequent screening.

cards.title: Fecal FIT Testing
cards.description: Learn about our in-house Fecal Immunochemical Test (FIT) — covered by all insurance providers. Annual FIT testing is recommended for all patients aged 45 and older, regardless of when their last colonoscopy was performed. Recent studies show yearly FIT is superior to Cologuard for colorectal cancer detection.

cards.title: Fecal FIT Instruction
cards.description: Step-by-step instructions for completing your Fecal Immunochemical Test (FIT) kit at home. Covers who should be screened, how to safely collect your stool sample using the provided collection paper, how to seal and return your kit within 48 hours, and what to expect from your results.

cards.title: Middletown Medical Laboratory Update
cards.description: Important updates from the Middletown Medical laboratory department. This document covers new lab services, updated protocols, specimen collection procedures, and standards that ensure accurate and timely results reported directly to your physician for the best possible patient care.

cards.title: MRI Instructions
cards.description: Prepare for your MRI exam with this comprehensive guide from our Radiology department. Includes a pre-exam metal implant safety checklist, clothing and jewelry recommendations, food and drink guidelines, contrast dye information, and tips for patients with claustrophobia. Contact Radiology at (845) 342-4774 ext. 4190 with any questions.

covid-19-monoclonal-antibodies.title: COVID-19 Monoclonal Antibodies
covid-19-monoclonal-antibodies.intro: Middletown Medical is proud to now offer monoclonal antibody treatments for COVID-19 patients.

paragraphs: Middletown Medical is proud to now offer monoclonal antibody treatments for COVID-19 patients. Early monoclonal antibody treatment has been proven to prevent hospitalization and reduce the risk of death. Recommended treatment is within 72 hours of COVID-19 diagnosis, and patients should inquire about treatment as soon as they receive a positive result. However, treatment can be given up to 10 days post-diagnosis.
Monoclonal antibodies are currently the only recognized treatment for COVID-19 to prevent hospitalization.
Middletown Medical's standard charge for this cutting-edge therapy is $2,250.

content.label: Schedule Appointment
content.note: Click here to request a Telemedicine appointment to verify your eligibility

items.question: What are monoclonal antibodies?
items.answer: Monoclonal antibodies are laboratory-produced molecules that act as substitute antibodies that can restore, enhance or mimic the immune system's attack on cells.

items.question: How do monoclonal antibodies treat COVID-19?
items.answer: Monoclonal antibodies for COVID-19 may block the virus that causes COVID-19 from attaching to human cells, making it more difficult for the virus to reproduce and cause harm. Monoclonal antibodies may also neutralize a virus.

items.question: How do I receive the treatment?
items.answer: Monoclonal antibody treatments for COVID-19 are only administered in settings in which healthcare providers have immediate access to medications to treat a severe infusion reaction, such as anaphylaxis, and have the ability to activate the emergency medical system (EMS). Middletown Medical's Infusion Center features the latest infusion treatment technology and has a specialized team to monitor your care during the antibody treatment.

covid-19-vaccine.title: Covid-19 Vaccine
covid-19-vaccine.intro: COVID-19 vaccines are now available at Middletown Medical. Protect yourself and your community by getting vaccinated at our convenient location.

paragraphs: *** We are not yet giving the Covid vaccine for under 12 years old. ***
LOCATION REMINDER: The vaccines will be given at 111 Maltese Drive, 1st Floor. Please do not go by the location in the actual appointment.
Vaccine Clinic Schedule — Covid Dose #1, Covid Dose #2, Covid Booster, Flu/Pneumo Clinic:

content.label: Schedule vaccine appointment
content.note: Opens registration in a new tab

items: We only give 12 years and older at 111 Maltese. 50 years and older are eligible for 3rd booster; it needs to be 4 months apart from the first booster.

Booster Eligibility for Pfizer/Moderna: May be given at least 5 months after the 2nd dose. Eligibility includes: 65 years of age and older; 18 through 64 years of age at high risk of severe COVID-19; 18 through 64 years of age with frequent institutional or occupational exposure to SARS-CoV-2.

Booster Eligibility for Janssen (Johnson and Johnson): May be given at least 2 months after the initial dose to recipients 18 years of age and older. Recipients may receive a single booster dose of Janssen COVID-19 Vaccine, Moderna or COVID-19 Vaccine or Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 Vaccine.

paragraphs: We are now offering the Moderna and Pfizer Booster for certain immunocompromised patients.
Call our main line at (845) 342-4774 or use the CHAT feature to get more info.
You can also get more info from the FDA website regarding COVID-19 vaccines: https://www.fda.gov/emergency-preparedness-and-response/coronavirus-disease-2019-covid-19/covid-19-vaccines

items.question: Step 1 — Check Your Eligibility
items.answer: The COVID-19 vaccine supply is currently limited and therefore subject to strict prioritization in accordance with Centers for Disease Control and Department of Health directives. As of April 6, 2021, all New Yorkers age 16 or older are eligible to be vaccinated. Please note that while New Yorkers age 16 and older are eligible to be vaccinated, at this time only the Pfizer vaccine is authorized for emergency use in people who are at least 16. Middletown Medical does not currently have access to the Pfizer vaccine and therefore is only offering vaccine appointments for those age 18 and older. Verify your eligibility by reviewing the entire NYS qualifications.

items.question: Step 2 — Complete the NYS Vaccination Form
items.answer: Complete the required NYS Vaccination form before your appointment. This ensures the vaccination process is efficient and that your records are properly filed with state health authorities.

items.question: Step 3 — Schedule Your Appointment
items.answer: Schedule your appointment with Middletown Medical in 2 convenient ways: Call our office directly at 845.342.4774, or schedule online through our patient portal.

sections.heading: How To Prepare For Your First Vaccine

paragraphs: 1. Complete your NYS Vaccine Form and be sure to bring it with you to your appointment.
2. Bring and wear a mask! You will be required to wear a mask during your vaccination.
3. Bring your ID, insurance, proof of residency, and proof of eligibility.
4. To help you feel your best — get a good night's rest, drink plenty of fluids, and make sure you have something to eat prior to your appointment time.
5. Bring your smartphone if you have one.
6. If possible, bring a friend or loved one with you.
7. Prepare your schedule for the 15-minute observation period following the vaccine.
8. Wear comfortable clothing that makes your arm easily accessible, such as a tee shirt.

post-covid-19-recovery.title: Post COVID-19 Recovery
post-covid-19-recovery.subtitle: Let Middletown Medical Help You Get on the Road to Recovery
post-covid-19-recovery.intro: For most patients who suffer from COVID-19, symptoms get better within a few weeks. However, some patients exhibit ongoing symptoms which may be mild or serious. These persistent symptoms can sometimes linger for months, and this stage of illness is referred to as 'long COVID', 'chronic COVID-19', or 'post-COVID syndrome'. Middletown Medical is here for you.

paragraphs: Our practice offers state-of-the-art testing to determine and develop treatment plans for any post-COVID symptoms you may be suffering from.

content.label: Schedule Appointment
content.note: Click on the button below to request a Telemedicine visit to discuss your post-COVID syndrome treatment options

items.question: What is post-COVID syndrome?
items.answer: Although most people with COVID-19 get better within weeks of illness, some people experience post-COVID conditions. Post-COVID conditions are a wide range of new, returning, or ongoing health problems people can experience more than four weeks after first being infected with the virus that causes COVID-19. Even people who did not have symptoms when they were infected can have post-COVID conditions. These conditions can have different types and combinations of health problems for different lengths of time.

items.question: What are the symptoms of post-COVID syndrome?
items.answer: These symptoms are not the same for everyone, but may include one or many of the following: Fevers; Fatigue; Shortness of breath; Exercise intolerance; Trouble thinking clearly, focusing, or remembering.

items.question: What should I do if I have symptoms of post-COVID syndrome?
items.answer: If you have any of the above lingering symptoms since your COVID-19 infection, it is important for you to schedule an appointment with Middletown Medical so we can develop your ongoing treatment plan. Our dedicated specialists are prepared to develop a customized plan for your post-COVID symptoms, including cardiac rehabilitation, pulmonary rehabilitation, physical therapy, and more.

content.label: Schedule Appointment
content.note: Click on the button below to request a Telemedicine visit to discuss your post-COVID syndrome treatment options

covid-19-info.title: Coronavirus (Covid-19) Information Center
covid-19-info.subtitle: Middletown Medical has become a regional leader for COVID-19 response.
covid-19-info.intro: Learn more below about how our team has risen to meet the challenges of the past year, and how we are keeping patients safe and cared for. Click here for information about the COVID-19 vaccine.

paragraphs: What is a novel coronavirus? A novel coronavirus is a new coronavirus that has not been previously identified. The virus causing coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) is not the same as the coronaviruses that commonly circulate among humans and cause mild illness, like the common cold.

How does the virus spread? The virus that causes COVID-19 most commonly spreads between people who are in close contact (within about 6 feet). It spreads through respiratory droplets or small particles produced when an infected person coughs, sneezes, sings, talks, or breathes. These particles can be inhaled into the nose, mouth, airways, and lungs. Droplets can also land on surfaces and be transferred by touch. There is growing evidence that droplets and airborne particles can remain suspended in the air and travel distances beyond 6 feet. Indoor environments without good ventilation increase this risk.

What is community spread? Community spread means people have been infected with the virus in an area, including some who are not sure how or where they became infected. Each health department determines community spread differently based on local conditions. Visit your health department's website for information on community spread in your area.

How can I protect myself? Wash your hands often; avoid close contact with people who are sick; maintain 6 feet of distance between yourself and people who don't live in your household; cover your mouth and nose with a mask when around others; cover coughs and sneezes; clean and disinfect regularly; monitor your health daily.

Is it safe to get care for my other medical conditions? Yes. It is important to continue taking care of your health and wellness. Continue your medications and do not change your treatment plan without talking to your healthcare provider. Have at least a 2-week supply of medications. Do not delay getting emergency care — if you need emergency help, call 911.

What should I do if I have had close contact with someone who has COVID-19? The best way to protect yourself and others is to stay home for 14 days if you think you've been exposed. Check your local health department's website for options to possibly shorten this quarantine period. Be alert for symptoms: fever, cough, shortness of breath, or other symptoms of COVID-19.

Should I get tested for COVID-19? If you have symptoms of COVID-19 and want to get tested, contact Middletown Medical today. CDC has guidance for who should be tested; decisions about testing are made by state and local health departments and healthcare providers. Visit our Covid-19 Testing page to learn more.
We are open and we are safe. We have implemented changes to protect our patients and staff:
Telemedicine — We are continuing our Telemedicine program and now offer TeleMedicine for Urgent Care. Call (845) 342-4774 to set up a visit.
Procedures — Our procedure suite is now open. If you have been putting off a needed medical procedure, please inquire with your doctor for scheduling.
Mask — We ask that each patient wear a mask or appropriate face covering (nose and mouth covered).

Remain in car — Unless it is a true hardship, we ask that guests remain in their cars when a patient comes in for a procedure or visit. You may be asked to wait in your car and be called on your cell phone to limit the number of people in our waiting area.
Social distancing — We have reduced and spaced chairs in waiting rooms to keep people safe.
3D Mammography — Moved to 60 Dunning Road at our Gynecology Office, Suite 3, for your convenience.
No outside gloves — We ask that no gloves from outside be worn in the buildings. Washing hands and using hand sanitizer is best.
Temperature check — All locations will check your temperature before you are admitted.
Drive-thru testing — We maintain COVID-19 testing locations with drive-thru service.
Disinfection — All imaging equipment and patient rooms are cleaned after each patient with appropriate sterilization supplies. We have increased disinfection at all facilities.
Sleep study reopened — Our advanced sleep lab is open. See one of our Pulmonologists for a prescription.
Staff safety — All staff temperatures are checked before each shift. All staff wear masks and clean work areas throughout the day. Medical representatives are not welcome at this time.

Clean your hands often. Wash your hands with soap and water for at least 20 seconds, especially after being in a public place or after blowing your nose, coughing, or sneezing. If soap and water are not available, use a hand sanitizer that contains at least 60% alcohol. Avoid touching your eyes, nose, and mouth with unwashed hands.
Learn more at cdc.gov.

telemedicine.title: Telemedicine
telemedicine.intro: All Middletown Medical providers now use Telemedicine. Urgent Care visits can be done through Telemedicine. Call (845) 342-4774 to set up a Telemedicine visit or chat with us. Connecting with your provider via your device has never been easier with our MM Telemedicine solution.

sections.heading: How to prepare for a Telemedicine visit

paragraphs: 1) Call your MMPC provider to request a Telemedicine appointment.
2) At the time of your appointment, your provider will text you a link to join the Telemedicine visit. It's that easy.
Some providers may choose to do your visit via WhatsApp. You can download WhatsApp on your phone if needed.

Same-day telemedicine appointments are available for: Primary / Family Medicine, Pediatrics, Allergy & Immunology, Cardiology, Dermatology, Diabetes In Children & Teens, Endocrinology, Gastroenterology, Gynecology, Infectious Diseases, Interventional Gastroenterology, Nephrology, Neurology, Orthopedics, Otolaryngology, Pain Management & Rehabilitation, Podiatry, Pulmonary Medicine, Rheumatology, Urology, Pediatric Endocrinology, Hematology & Oncology, and Optometry.

Wellness visits with your PCP; medication management and prescription renewal; minor urgent care (e.g. pink eye, fevers, rashes); birth control counseling; chronic condition management; pediatrics; post-hospital discharge; post-surgical follow-up.
Call (845) 342-4774 and press 2 to set up a Telemedicine visit.

New HIPAA Guidance authorizes the use of Telemedicine: waivers of originating and geographic site restrictions on Medicare telehealth services (delivery in all areas including patients' homes); expanded telehealth for new and established patients for COVID-19 and other conditions; permission for providers to use everyday communications technologies such as FaceTime or Skype during the COVID-19 public health emergency without HIPAA penalties.

patient-centered-medical-home.title: Patient Centered Medical Home Certification
patient-centered-medical-home.intro: Middletown Medical is your Patient Centered Medical Home. We provide comprehensive, compassionate and continuous care for pediatric to adult medicine.

sections.heading: What type of services does my Medical Home provide for me and my family?

paragraphs: Same day appointments, extended hours, and urgent care support.
Preventive care and physicals (health risk assessments, sports and school physicals).
Acute care for illness and injuries.
Management of multi-specialty care plans including mental health.
Well child visits, screening and vaccinations.
24×7 phone access to your care team.
Online electronic access to your medical records (healow™).
Referrals to top specialists and mental health providers.

You don't need insurance to be seen at our clinic. If you do have insurance, please bring your information with you. If you do not have insurance, we still want to see you — we have staff that will assist you in signing up for insurance.

Middletown Medical wants to partner with our patients to provide up-to-date health information and recommends that you participate in self-management and support activities offered in the community. We provide direct communication at your visit, via phone or the patient portal. You may be asked by your provider to complete a Goal & Self-Improvement form during your visit to help us understand where you would like to focus your care. We adhere to evidence-based medicine and encourage you to review the helpful websites below. Multiple languages are provided at national sites.

billing-insurance.title: Billing Department
billing-insurance.subtitle: Understanding Your Insurance & Billing
billing-insurance.intro: Continuous changes with health insurance plans makes it extremely important to understand your coverage, including the costs you must pay when visiting your physician or when having tests and procedures. Middletown Medical recommends that you learn the specifics of your plan before your next appointment.

paragraphs: Continuous changes with health insurance plans makes it extremely important to understand your coverage, including the costs you must pay when visiting your physician or when having tests and procedures. For this reason, Middletown Medical recommends that you learn the specifics of your plan before your appointment. For example, your health insurance plan may require multiple copays, higher deductibles, and coinsurance. Coverage will depend on the type of plan you have chosen.

We accept most in tier insurance plans. Call our offices for the most current information or if you don't see your insurance plan listed below. Participating providers subject to change without notice.

paragraphs: We now accept payments from CareCredit®. What is CareCredit®?
CareCredit® is a health, wellness, and personal care credit card that helps patients and clients get the care they need and want, without delaying appointments or treatment.
Cardholders can use CareCredit promotional financing to pay over time for deductibles, copays, and procedures not covered by insurance,* while you pay in two business days.
Visit www.carecredit.com for more information.

To learn the details of your individual health care policy, you can visit your insurance company's website, review your member booklet or contact your company's customer services department. The back of your insurance card should include the phone numbers and website information.
Some key information you need before your visit:

items: Is my doctors also covered completely? If not, do I have a copay or deductible amount to meet?
If I need any tests, will they be covered completely? If no, how much will I be expected to pay?
If I need a procedure, including surgery, how much will my insurance cover? How much will I be expected to pay?
All I need authorization before my health care visit?

items.question: What is a deductible?
items.answer:
A deductible is the amount of "out-of-pocket" costs that you pay before your insurance plan begins reimbursement. A deductible is usually a set dollar amount, such as $250 or $500, but can be higher based on the policy you have chosen.

If your insurance plan has a deductible, you will be required to pay an estimate of your portion of the bill at the time of service.

items.question: What is co-insurance
items.answer:
Co-insurance is your share of the costs of a healthcare service. It is usually figured as a percentage of the total charges for the services. You start paying coinsurance after you've paid your plan's deductible.

For example, if you've paid your $1,500 deductible for the year, and your plan covers 70% of a particular cost, then you're responsible to pay the other 30% — that's the co-insurance.

It is in your best interest to understand what your insurance does and does not cover, examples co-pays, deductible and co-insurance, what you are responsible for, and what we require from you at the time services are provided to you at Middletown Medical.

items.question: What types of insurances does Middletown Medical accept?
items.answer: We accept many types of health insurance plans: Medicare, Medicare Advantage Plans, HMO, PPO, and Managed Care plans. For a list of all of them CLICK HERE.

items.question: What is a co-payment?
items.answer:
A co-pay (co-payment) is the portion of your healthcare expenses not covered by insurance. It is the amount your insurance company states is your responsibility for each service you have rendered and is required to be paid at the time of treatment.

Your co-pay information may be listed on your insurance card as the following: PCP, Specialist, Urgent Care, ER. There also may be separate co-pays for each type of service.

Examples: Office visit, Lab/blood work, Diagnostic testing (x-ray).

items.question: Who is responsible for paying my bill?
items.answer:
You are responsible for your co-pay, co-insurance and/or deductible at the time of service.

Middletown Medical will bill your insurance company directly on your behalf unless you specify otherwise; however, you are ultimately responsible for making sure that your bill is paid.

If a balance remains after your insurance has issued a payment, you will receive a bill asking for payment; this bill is due immediately upon receipt of your statement. We accept cash, checks, money orders and credit cards.

In circumstances when you will be unable to make monthly payments to Middletown Medical until your bill is paid in full. You should discuss this with our Billing Department by calling 845-342-5199 (from weekdays from 8:30 a.m. to 4 p.m.)

items.question: Beneficiary
items.answer: A person who receives benefits of any insurance plan or policy.

items.question: Explanation of Benefits (EOB)
items.answer: A statement sent by the insurance company showing the processing of a claim.

items.question: Claim
items.answer: A request for payment for services submitted by the provider.

items.question: Medically Necessary
items.answer: Treatments or services that insurance policies will pay for as defined in the contract.

items.question: Co-insurance
items.answer: A specified percentage of covered expenses which the insurance carrier requires the beneficiary to pay toward eligible medical bills.

items.question: Co-pay or Co-payment
items.answer: A specific set dollar amount contracted between the insurance company and the beneficiary to be paid prior to any services rendered.

items.question: Covered Services
items.answer: Services for which an insurance policy will pay.

items.question: Deductible
items.answer: A specified dollar amount of medical expenses that the beneficiary must pay before an insurance policy will pay.

items.question: Non-Covered Services
items.answer: Services for which an insurance policy will not provide payment. These services are to be paid by the patient at the time of service.

items.question: Pre-Certification/Authorization
items.answer: A service-specific requirement that your insurance company's approval be obtained before a medical service is provided.

items.question: Provider
items.answer: A person or organization that provides medical services.

patient-bill-of-rights.title: Patient Bill of Rights
patient-bill-of-rights.intro: Your rights as a patient at Middletown Medical are important to us. The full document is available as a PDF — it opens in a new tab so you can read or print it without leaving this site.

paragraphs: You have the right to receive considerate and respectful care, to be informed about your treatment, to privacy and confidentiality, and to voice concerns without affecting your care.

content.label: Open Patient Bill of Rights (PDF)
content.note: Opens in a new browser tab

findhelp.title: FindHelp.org
findhelp.intro: Community resources for food, housing, transportation, and other social needs. Use the button below to open FindHelp.org in a new tab — you stay on our site until you choose to continue.

paragraphs: Middletown Medical may refer patients to FindHelp.org to connect with local programs that support health and well-being outside the clinic.

media-center.title: Media Center
media-center.subtitle: News & updates
media-center.intro: Stay informed about Middletown Medical services, community initiatives, and health information. For press or media inquiries, contact our main line at (845) 342-4774.

paragraphs: For downloadable guides, forms, and clinical updates, visit our Patient Resources section.
